entries.name: RTK
entries.description: Compresses command output (git, tests, builds) before Claude reads it

steps.note: rtk's PreToolUse hook rewrites a matched command to `rtk <cmd>` (bare name, no path) via Claude Code's updatedInput. The hook itself is injected with an absolute path, but that rewrite is not, so ${PIGGY_BIN} must be on PATH or every rewritten command dies with `command not found: rtk`. Appends an idempotent, delimited block to the shell profile (~/.zshrc); reversed by remove_dir_from_path.

VERIFIED 2026-07-12 against rtk v0.43.0: `rtk init -g --auto-patch` writes exactly this shape (matcher Bash, command `rtk hook claude`, no timeout field). Piggy injects the same object with ${PIGGY_BIN}/rtk expanded to the absolute pinned-binary path, via its own merge engine (Piggy does not run `rtk init`). rtk init additionally writes ~/.claude/RTK.md, an @RTK.md line in CLAUDE.md, and ~/Library/Application Support/rtk/filters.toml; Piggy deliberately creates none of those (the hook alone delivers the compression, and not touching user-authored CLAUDE.md keeps uninstall byte-identical), so Piggy's footprint is exactly this hook plus the binary.
rtk is the only binary saver, so removing the PATH block on uninstall is safe. Revisit if a second ${PIGGY_BIN} binary saver is added.

entries.claimedSavings: ~80% on shell output (author estimate, TypeScript/Rust projects)
entries.notes: Hook only intercepts the Bash tool; Read/Grep/Glob untouched. rtk telemetry is opt-in only (off by default unless the user turns it on), so Piggy's hook runs it with no telemetry and sets nothing extra.

entries.name: Caveman
entries.description: Claude answers in short caveman speak — fewer words, same code

configOptions.label: Intensity
configOptions.description: How compressed Claude's replies are. Applies from the next session.

choices.label: Full
choices.description: Classic caveman: drops articles, fragments OK

apply.note: The plugin's documented user config (resolution order: env var, repo-local, this file, built-in 'full'). Piggy sets only defaultMode and preserves every other field; the plugin re-reads it at each session start.

entries.warning: Independent JetBrains A/B test (82–86 tasks) measured ~8.5% real-world savings vs the 65% claim — with no quality loss. Modest but real.
entries.notes: Skill adds ~1–1.5k input tokens per turn (author's own honest caveat). Zero telemetry.

entries.name: Ponytail
entries.description: Nudges Claude to build only what you asked for — no gold-plating

steps.reason: lifecycle hooks; degrades to skill-only without it

steps.note: must run BEFORE plugin uninstall — cleans ~/.config/ponytail and any statusLine entry

entries.warning: Changes how Claude writes code (less of it). Authors retracted their early 80–94% claim themselves; current honest benchmark: −22% tokens.
entries.notes: Node.js soft dependency. Authors publicly self-corrected inflated benchmarks — good honesty signal.

entries.name: Sweep
entries.description: Finds add-ons you never use that cost tokens on every request, and switches them off
entries.claimedSavings: depends on your setup (Piggy measures it)
entries.notes: Piggy's own code. Static-config cleanup only; every disable is one-click reversible.

entries.name: Claude Token Optimizer
entries.description: Restructures a project's CLAUDE.md so sessions start lighter

steps.note: per-project npx run; needs project picker UI. Its hook templates are NOT installed — Piggy's merge engine would own that if ever enabled.

entries.notes: v1.1: per-project operation doesn't fit the v1 global-toggle UX yet.

entries.name: Token Optimizer
entries.description: Sends Claude only what changed in files it already saw
entries.licenseNote: Source-available, NOT open source. Free for individuals and small teams (<5 people or <$20k/mo). Piggy shows this label before you turn it on.

steps.reason: the plugin's compression hooks are Python; without python3 they no-op and save nothing

entries.notes: VERIFIED 2026-07-16 against plugin v5.11.50: the plugin ships its own hooks/hooks.json (PreToolUse Read/Bash/Agent/mcp, PreCompact, SessionStart), which Claude Code loads when the plugin is enabled, so Piggy owns no settings.json hooks for it and enabling is enough for it to work. The author's `/token-optimizer` slash command is optional one-time onboarding (audit + dashboard), not required for the saving hooks. Hooks are Python-based and degrade to a no-op if python3 is absent (the soft require_binary above only warns).

entries.name: Context Mode
entries.description: Keeps huge tool outputs out of Claude's context until needed
entries.licenseNote: Source-available, NOT open source. Fine for personal use; restricts hosted resale. Piggy shows this label before install.

steps.note: bundles an MCP server + 6 hook events; uninstall path unverified upstream — must be empirically tested before enabling.

entries.claimedSavings: 98% on sandboxed tool output (author; unverified 'used at' logo wall — ignore it)

entries.name: Headroom
entries.description: Compresses everything Claude reads. Works in sessions you start with piggy-claude.

steps.note: Isolated venv so Piggy never touches the user's global site-packages; uninstall deletes the whole tree.
so the piggy-claude launcher resolves by bare name. Shared with rtk; the PATH line is removed only when the last ${PIGGY_BIN} consumer is uninstalled.

steps.name: piggy-claude
steps.note: WRAPPER MODEL (chosen deliberately): compression is scoped to sessions the user starts with `piggy-claude`, which runs `headroom wrap claude`. Piggy sets NO global ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL and runs NO persistent proxy daemon — headroom's proxy lives and dies with the wrapped session — so a stopped proxy can never break normal `claude`. The trade-off vs an always-on default is explicit and reversible.
conditional: kept if rtk (or any other ${PIGGY_BIN} saver) is still installed.

entries.notes: Piggy's default compressor (defaultOn), installed as an isolated Python venv + a `piggy-claude` launcher. Wraps rtk internally, so it conflictsWith rtk — turning Headroom on auto-disables rtk and vice versa. Also conflictsWith nadirclaw: both are proxies and can't both own ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL. Uses the wrapper-launcher model rather than a global proxy: run `piggy-claude` to compress a session; plain `claude` is untouched.

entries.name: NadirClaw
entries.description: Sends simple prompts to cheap/local models and hard ones to Claude, through a local router proxy
entries.licenseNote: Source-available, NOT open source. Free for noncommercial use; commercial use needs a license from getnadir.com. Piggy shows this label before install.

steps.note: pip install nadirclaw, then `nadirclaw claude onboard` wires ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL/ANTHROPIC_MODEL to the local router. Needs the same v2 proxy install engine as headroom (pip + proxy lifecycle + settings env wiring); no proxy/pip step kind exists yet, so this is refused as not-installable today.

entries.warning: Routes some prompts to cheaper/local models, so answer quality (not just token count) can change. Measure before trusting it.
entries.claimedSavings: 40–70% by routing to cheaper models (author, getnadir.com)
entries.notes: LLM router / proxy. Sets ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL + ANTHROPIC_MODEL via `nadirclaw claude onboard`. conflictsWith headroom — two proxies can't both own ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL. Does NOT conflict with rtk (rtk's Bash-output hook is an orthogonal layer). Repo NadirRouter/NadirClaw; pip package `nadirclaw`.

entries.name: token-optimizer-mcp
entries.description: MCP server with 65 tools + hook pipeline
entries.exclusionReason: No documented uninstall path (violates Piggy's reversibility principle); npm postinstall auto-edits settings.json without opt-in; self-documented settings.json corruption bug; releases frozen ~8 months. Shown in Discovered tab with this explanation — never installable until fixed upstream.
